items.title: Iran fires on ships in Strait of Hormuz as it closes the critical chokepoint again
items.standfirst: Iran swiftly reversed course on reopening the Strait of Hormuz after the U.S. said the move would not end its blockade.
items.content: Iran swiftly reversed course on reopening the Strait of Hormuz after the U.S. said the move would not end its blockade.

items.title: Trump shifts message on Iran war
items.standfirst: President Trump said “very good conversations” are taking place with Iran after warning the U.S. may start “dropping bombs again” if no deal is met by next week when the ceasefire with Iran expires. It comes as Iran is declaring the Strait of Hormuz closed in response to the U.S. continuing its blockade of Iranian ports. NBC News’ Julie Tsikrin reports.
items.content: President Trump said “very good conversations” are taking place with Iran after warning the U.S. may start “dropping bombs again” if no deal is met by next week when the ceasefire with Iran expires. It comes as Iran is declaring the Strait of Hormuz closed in response to the U.S. continuing its blockade of Iranian ports. NBC News’ Julie Tsikrin reports.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz closed again, Iran says, as ships attacked
items.standfirst: Iran is blaming a US blockade for the closure, saying it breaches the ceasefire reached between the two countries.
items.content: Iran is blaming a US blockade for the closure, saying it breaches the ceasefire reached between the two countries.

items.title: Iran says it has closed the Strait of Hormuz again, citing U.S. blockade
items.standfirst: Iran said on Saturday that it closed the Strait of Hormuz again amid the ongoing U.S. blockade of its ports in the waterway. President Trump said Friday that the waterway was "completely open."
items.content: Iran said on Saturday that it closed the Strait of Hormuz again amid the ongoing U.S. blockade of its ports in the waterway. President Trump said Friday that the waterway was "completely open."

items.title: Iran closes strait of Hormuz again ‘until US lifts blockade’
items.standfirst: IRGC reportedly fires on tanker as it tries to pass through strait during brief window when shipping lane had reopened• Middle East crisis – live updatesIranian officials say they have reversed the reopening of the strait of Hormuz and reimposed restrictions on the vital shipping lane after the US said it would not end its blockade of Iranian ports.A UK maritime agency reported that Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) ships had fired at a tanker as it attempted to pass through the strait on Saturday. Reuters reported an Indian-flagged vessel carrying crude oil had also been attacked while in the waterway. Continue reading...
items.content: IRGC reportedly fires on tanker as it tries to pass through strait during brief window when shipping lane had reopened• Middle East crisis – live updatesIranian officials say they have reversed the reopening of the strait of Hormuz and reimposed restrictions on the vital shipping lane after the US said it would not end its blockade of Iranian ports.A UK maritime agency reported that Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) ships had fired at a tanker as it attempted to pass through the strait on Saturday. Reuters reported an Indian-flagged vessel carrying crude oil had also been attacked while in the waterway. Continue reading...

items.content: Two Indian ships crossing the Strait of Hormuz have come under fire.

items.title: Iran says it has closed the Strait of Hormuz again, as ceasefire nears its end
items.standfirst: Iran's military said on Saturday the Strait of Hormuz has "returned to its previous state." The announcement came after President Trump had said the blockade on Iranian ports would remain in place.
items.content: Iran's military said on Saturday the Strait of Hormuz has "returned to its previous state." The announcement came after President Trump had said the blockade on Iranian ports would remain in place.(Image credit: Win McNamee)

items.title: Iran reimposes 'strict control' over Strait of Hormuz, citing continued U.S. naval blockade
items.standfirst: Iranian military officials said Saturday that conditions in the Strait of Hormuz have reverted to “strict control,” citing the continued U.S. blockade of its ports, just one day after declaring the waterway “completely open.”“Control over the Strait of Hormuz has returned to its previous state, and this strategic waterway is now under strict management and control of the armed forces,” a spokesperson for Iran’s Khatam al-Anbiya military command headquarters said.“Until America al

items.content: A gunman has killed six people and injured at least 14 in a mass shooting in Ukraine's capital before he was shot and killed by police.(Image credit: Dan Bashakov)

clusters.title: French UNIFIL soldier killed in Lebanon, Hezbollah responsible, says Macron
clusters.neutral_headline: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
bullet_summary: In calls with Lebanese President ⁠Joseph Aoun and Prime Minister Nawaf Salam, French President Emmanuel Macron condemned the "unacceptable attack", his ‌office said in a statement
Reported by France24, BBC World

items.title: French UNIFIL soldier killed in Lebanon, Hezbollah responsible, says Macron
items.standfirst: French President Emmanuel Macron announced the death of a French UN peacekeeping soldier Saturday following an attack that also left three people wounded in southern Lebanon, saying that "everything suggests" Hezbollah being responsible for the attack. The Iran-backed group denied involvement.
items.content: French President Emmanuel Macron announced the death of a French UN peacekeeping soldier Saturday following an attack that also left three people wounded in southern Lebanon, saying that "everything suggests" Hezbollah being responsible for the attack. The Iran-backed group denied involvement.

items.title: UNIFIL sheds light on southern Lebanon attack that killed French soldier
items.standfirst: A French soldier was killed in southern Lebanon on Saturday 18, while serving with UNIFIL, the United Nations peacekeeping force. UNIFIL deputy spokesperson Tilak Pokharel was FRANCE 24’s guest this afternoon to explain what happened and what is currently known about those responsible.
items.content: A French soldier was killed in southern Lebanon on Saturday 18, while serving with UNIFIL, the United Nations peacekeeping force. UNIFIL deputy spokesperson Tilak Pokharel was FRANCE 24’s guest this afternoon to explain what happened and what is currently known about those responsible.

items.title: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
items.standfirst: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.
items.content: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.

items.title: French soldier killed in attack on UN mission in southern Lebanon, officials say
items.standfirst: A French soldier was killed and three others wounded ​while clearing a road in southern Lebanon in an attack that UNIFIL peacekeepers and French officials said on Saturday was likely carried out ​by ‌Iran-backed Hezbollah. In calls with Lebanese President ⁠Joseph Aoun and Prime Minister Nawaf Salam, French President Emmanuel Macron condemned the "unacceptable attack", his ‌office said in a statement. Renée Davis, FRANCE 24's correspondent, has the latest from Beyrouth.
items.content: A French soldier was killed and three others wounded ​while clearing a road in southern Lebanon in an attack that UNIFIL peacekeepers and French officials said on Saturday was likely carried out ​by ‌Iran-backed Hezbollah. In calls with Lebanese President ⁠Joseph Aoun and Prime Minister Nawaf Salam, French President Emmanuel Macron condemned the "unacceptable attack", his ‌office said in a statement. Renée Davis, FRANCE 24's correspondent, has the latest from Beyrouth.

bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, CBS News

items.title: Two US army soldiers injured by brown bear during training exercise in Alaska
items.standfirst: Soldiers were participating in ‘land navigation training’ when bear injured them in a ‘defensive attack’Two US army soldiers have been injured after encountering a brown bear in a mountainous training area in Anchorage, Alaska, the military said on Friday.The incident happened on Thursday as the soldiers were participating in a “land navigation training event” in Arctic valley, part of the joint base Elmendorf-Richardson’s training area. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

clusters.title: U.S. sanctions two firms over allegedly recruiting Colombian mercenaries in Sudan war
clusters.neutral_headline: Sudan’s war leaves thousands of children alone and displaced
bullet_summary: Reported by NBC World, France24

items.title: U.S. sanctions two firms over allegedly recruiting Colombian mercenaries in Sudan war
items.standfirst: CAIRO — The U.S. imposed sanctions on three people and two firms over allegedly recruiting and deploying Colombian mercenaries to fight alongside the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces in Sudan's war, which has entered its fourth year with no end in sight
items.content: CAIRO — The U.S. imposed sanctions on three people and two firms over allegedly recruiting and deploying Colombian mercenaries to fight alongside the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces in Sudan's war, which has entered its fourth year with no end in sight

items.title: Sudan’s war leaves thousands of children alone and displaced
items.standfirst: Sudan is entering its fourth year of civil war between the army and the Rapid Support Forces, a conflict the UN calls the world’s largest humanitarian crisis, with children among the main victims. In Darfur and beyond, thousands are displaced, many separated from their families, facing hunger, violence and deep trauma despite overwhelmed aid efforts.
items.content: Sudan is entering its fourth year of civil war between the army and the Rapid Support Forces, a conflict the UN calls the world’s largest humanitarian crisis, with children among the main victims. In Darfur and beyond, thousands are displaced, many separated from their families, facing hunger, violence and deep trauma despite overwhelmed aid efforts.

items.title: Sudan war enters fourth year as the humanitarian crisis deepens
items.standfirst: Sudan has entered its fourth year of devastating conflict, after fighting broke out in April 2023 between the Sudanese army and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces. The war has left millions displaced and, according to the UN, created the world’s largest humanitarian crisis.
items.content: Sudan has entered its fourth year of devastating conflict, after fighting broke out in April 2023 between the Sudanese army and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces. The war has left millions displaced and, according to the UN, created the world’s largest humanitarian crisis.

clusters.title: Oil price drops below $90 a barrel after Iran says strait of Hormuz is open
clusters.neutral_headline: Oil price drops below $90 a barrel after Iran says strait of Hormuz is open
bullet_summary: Iran’s foreign minister said vessels would be free to transit the strait of Hormuz for the duration of the 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, which was struck on Thursday
stock indexes hit all-time highs after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz was open for commercial vessels

items.title: Oil price drops below $90 a barrel after Iran says strait of Hormuz is open
items.standfirst: Gas price also falls and markets rise amid hopes Donald Trump will succeed in reaching deal with TehranOil and gas prices fell sharply on Friday after Iran said the strait of Hormuz was open to commercial shipping, potentially clearing the way for tankers holding millions of barrels of oil and gas to reach the global market.Iran’s foreign minister said vessels would be free to transit the strait of Hormuz for the duration of the 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, which was struck on Thursday. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Oil plunges to lowest price in weeks after Iran says it will fully open Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: Oil prices plunged and two U.S. stock indexes hit all-time highs after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz was open for commercial vessels.
items.content: Oil prices plunged and two U.S. stock indexes hit all-time highs after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz was open for commercial vessels.
